uk: Свв. двадцяти тисяч мучеників, спалених у Нікомидії
Блаженного священномученика Григорія Хомишина, єпископа Станіславівського.
saints.en: Sts. Twenty Thousand Martyrs burned at Nicomedia. Blessed Hieromartyr Hryhoriy Khomyshyn, Bishop of Stanyslaviv

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизми нема. На “Господи, взиваю” – 6 стихир: 3 свята, 3 мчч., Сл., І нині: свята. Стиховня свята. По “Отче наш”: тропар мчч., Сл., І нині: свята. Відпуст середн. з помин. свята і мчч.
Утреня: по “Бог Господь”: тропар свята (2), Сл.: тр. мчч., І нині: тр. свята. Катизми черг., сідал. свята. Пс. 50. Канон свята та мчч. Катавасія тільки по 3-ій, 6-ій, 8-ій та 9-ій піснях – ірмос канону мчч. По 3-ій – конд.-ікос і сідал. мчч, Сл., І нині: свята. По 6-ій – конд.-ікос свята. По 9-ій – світил. мчч. (2), Сл., І нині: Світил. свята. Стиховня свята. По “Отче наш”: тропар мчч., Сл., І нині: свята. Відпуст середній.
Часи: на всіх – тропар свята; на 3 та 9 – Сл.: тр. мчч. Кондаки: 1 та 6 – свята; 3 та 9 – мчч.
Літургія: антифони: перші два – свята, третій – загальний з приспівом свята; “Прийдіте, поклонімся”. Тропар свята, Сл., І нині: конд. свята; Прокімен, алилуя і причасний свята. Ап. – 1 Пт. 59 зач.; 2, 21-3.9. Єв. – Мр. 48 зач.; 10, 46-52. Замість “Достойно” – ірмос 9-ї пісні канону свята з його приспівом. Відпуст з помин. свята і мчч.

troparion_en.text: Your Nativity, O Christ our God,  /  has shone to the world the Light of knowledge;  /  for by it, those who worshipped the stars / were taught by a star to adore You,  / the Sun of Righteousness,1  /  and to know You, the Dayspring2 from on High.  / O Lord, glory to You!

kontakion_en.text:
(Original Melody)
Today the Virgin gives birth to the Transcendent One,  /  and the earth offers a cave to the Unapproachable One!  / Angels with shepherds glorify Him.  /  The wise men journey with a star,  /  since for our sake the Pre-Eternal God was born as a young Child.3

matched_oca_saint: Afterfeast of the Nativity of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ

oca_saints.name: Afterfeast of the Nativity of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ

troparion.text: Your Nativity, O Christ our God,  /  has shone to the world the Light of knowledge;  /  for by it, those who worshipped the stars / were taught by a star to adore You,  / the Sun of Righteousness,1  /  and to know You, the Dayspring2 from on High.  / O Lord, glory to You!

kontakion.text:
(Original Melody)
Today the Virgin gives birth to the Transcendent One,  /  and the earth offers a cave to the Unapproachable One!  / Angels with shepherds glorify Him.  /  The wise men journey with a star,  /  since for our sake the Pre-Eternal God was born as a young Child.3

troparion.text: Blessed is the earth that received your blood, passion-bearers of the Lord, / and holy is the dwelling place which received your spirits. / You triumphed over the enemy in the stadium / and you preached Christ with boldness. / Since He is good, we pray that you beseech Him to save our souls.

kontakion.text: Their souls strengthened by faith, the twenty thousand martyrs accepted their suffering by fire, / and cried out to You, the One born of the Virgin: / “Like gold, myrrh, and frankincense, the gifts of the Persian kings, / receive our whole burnt offering, O Eternal God.”

oca_saints.name: Venerable Ignatius of Loma and Yaroslav

troparion.text:
(Podoben: “Come, let us worship the Word...”)
With the streams of your tears, you cultivated the barrenness of the desert,  / and by your sighings from the depths, your labors bore fruit a hundredfold,  /  and you became a luminary of the world, radiant with miracles, O Ignatius our Father.  / Intercede with Christ God for the salvation of our souls.

kontakion.text:
(Podoben: “You sought the heights...”)
Having divinely armed yourself with purity of soul  /  and firmly grasping unceasing prayer as a spear, you have slain the armies of the demons.  / Therefore, we beseech you, O Father Ignatius:  /  “Always intercede for those who honor you.”

oca_saints.name: Apostle Nicanor the Deacon of the Seventy

troparion.text: Holy Apostle Nicanor, / entreat the merciful God, / to grant our souls forgiveness of transgressions.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Simon the Myrrh-gusher of Mount Athos

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Simon, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

By sharing in the ways of the Apostles, / you became a successor to their throne. / Through the practice of virtue, / you found the way to divine contemplation, O inspired one of God; / by teaching the word of truth without error, / you defended the Faith, even to the shedding of your blood. / Hieromartyr Νikόdēmos, entreat Christ God to save our souls.
